message:
fix(web): prevent session DoS from empty requests and handle empty multipart bodies

Two web-layer hardening fixes:

1. session.rs — Untouched new sessions were being saved to the store and
   getting a Set-Cookie header, meaning any unauthenticated request would
   create server-side session state. An attacker could exhaust the session
   store by flooding requests without cookies. Now: sessions that are both
   new AND unmodified are silently discarded (no store.save, no Set-Cookie).
   Also fixes the cleared-session flow to skip store.delete and cookie
   expiry for sessions that were never persisted (is_new + cleared).

2. multipart.rs — parse_multipart() did not handle the case where the
   first boundary is immediately followed by the close delimiter ('--'),
   producing spurious parse errors on valid empty multipart bodies. Now
   checks for the close delimiter after the initial boundary and returns
   an empty fields vec.

New test: tests/session_dos_test.rs verifies that an empty request through
SessionMiddleware does not persist anything to the store.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
